sanskrit:
दातव्यमिति यद्दानं दीयतेऽनुपकारिणे।
देशे काले च पात्रे च तद्दानं सात्त्विकं स्मृतम्॥
transliteration:
dātavyam iti yad dānaṃ dīyate ’nupakāriṇe |
deśe kāle ca pātre ca tad dānaṃ sāttvikaṃ smṛtam ||
literal_meaning: That gift which is given out of duty, at the proper time and place, to a worthy person, and without expectation of return, is considered to be charity in the mode of goodness.
contextual_meaning: Healthy giving is intentional, boundary-aware, and free of covert contracts; it supports both giver and receiver without depleting self-worth.
psychological_state: Secure attachment: the giver maintains self-esteem while offering help without unconsciously seeking approval or control.
trigger_scenario: You volunteer for a cause that matches your values, set a time limit, and decline requests that exceed your bandwidth without feeling selfish.
anchor_text: Give only when it helps without strings or self-harm.
